items.q: How do you say 'I do not eat' in French?
items.why: Students forget the verb 'sandwich'. Ne and pas wrap AROUND the verb in present tense - ne BEFORE the verb, pas AFTER. In compound tenses, they wrap around the auxiliary.

items.q: How do you say 'I was using my computer when the phone rang' in French?
items.correct: J'utilisais mon ordinateur quand le téléphone a sonné.
items.misconception: J'ai utilisé mon ordinateur quand le téléphone a sonné.
items.random_wrong: J'utilise mon ordinateur quand le téléphone sonne.
items.why: Students use passé composé for both verbs. The rule: ONGOING action (was using) = IMPARFAIT; INTERRUPTING action (rang) = PASSÉ COMPOSÉ. This tense pairing is critical for narrative writing.
Students assume librairie = library. It actually means BOOKSHOP. The French word for library is bibliothèque. Both relate to books but serve different purposes.
The answer examiners want is: President of Senegal. “French PM” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.
It's Cultural mixing. “Cultural purity” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Year Senghor became Senegal's first president?
items.why: Exact answer: 1960. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
